mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending monetary easing, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances or out-of-sample t-statistic falls below 1.5 over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate collapsing aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive forward returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above its 2017 baseline during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or returns are negative in > 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 305 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 305 or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.0 over 36-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate signals robust labor market expansion that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in > 50% of observations or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth expands the economic base and increases corporate revenue expectations, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 98 during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply-side contraction that bottlenecks production and erodes profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns with accuracy < 50% when tested on out-of-sample data from 2015-2023 or t-statistic falls below 1.0.
